pi_items.source: Task description in task.json confirms agent must 'Save the corrected, standards-compliant document as /home/ga/Documents/commercial_lease_final.odt'
Task description in task.json states 'The draft is at /home/ga/Documents/draft_commercial_lease.odt' and setup_task.sh creates file at this exact path

Task description in task.json states 'The firm's document standards guide is at /home/ga/Documents/firm_standards.txt' and setup_task.sh copies it from /workspace/tasks/legal_contract_styles/assets/firm_standards.txt to this location

Code analysis of setup_task.sh shows exactly 10 fake_h1() calls corresponding to sections 1-10 ('1. PARTIES' through '10. DEFAULT, REMEDIES, AND DISPUTE RESOLUTION'). Task metadata requires ≥9, so actual count of 10 meets requirement.

Code analysis of setup_task.sh shows 27 fake_h2() calls: Section 1 has 3 subsections (1.1-1.3), Section 2 has 3 (2.1-2.3), Section 3 has 3 (3.1-3.3), Section 4 has 3 (4.1-4.3), Section 5 has 2 (5.1-5.2), Section 6 has 3 (6.1-6.3), Section 7 has 2 (7.1-7.2), Section 8 has 2 (8.1-8.2), Section 9 has 3 (9.1-9.3), Section 10 has 3 (10.1-10.3). Total: 3+3+3+3+2+3+2+2+3+3 = 27. Metadata requirement of ≥18 is met.

firm_standards.txt Section 2.1 requires 'Every agreement of five pages or more MUST include an automatically generated Table of Contents'. Export script checks for 'text:table-of-content' element and verifier awards 25 points for this.

firm_standards.txt Section 2.2 states 'All pages after the title page must display a page number' and requires 'Page numbers must appear in the document FOOTER'. Export script checks for 'text:page-number' field and verifier awards 15 points for this.

setup_task.sh content section explicitly includes body text: 'Pacific Properties LLC, a California limited liability company ("Landlord")'. README.md also explicitly states 'Landlord: Pacific Properties LLC (fictitious but realistic California LLC)'

setup_task.sh content section explicitly includes body text: 'Meridian Analytics Inc., a Delaware corporation authorized to do business in the State of California ("Tenant")'. README.md also explicitly states 'Tenant: Meridian Analytics Inc. (fictitious but realistic California tech company)'

pi_items.metadata_value: 4500 Technology Drive, Suite 200, Palo Alto, CA 94304
pi_items.verified_value: 4500 Technology Drive, Suite 200, Palo Alto, CA 94304
pi_items.source: setup_task.sh content section explicitly includes: 'Suite 200, located on the second floor of the building known as Palo Alto Technology Center, situated at 4500 Technology Drive, Palo Alto, California 94304 (the "Building")'. README states the property address is 'real street' but web searches for '4500 Technology Drive Palo Alto CA' and 'Palo Alto Technology Center 4500 Technology Drive' found no evidence this specific address exists. The actual Palo Alto Technology Center is located at 1800 Embarcadero Road, not 4500 Technology Drive. Address appears to be fictitious or synthetic.

privileged_info_summary: This task uses synthetic data generated by setup_task.sh. The draft commercial lease document is dynamically created with 10 main sections formatted as fake headings and 27 subsections, requiring conversion to proper ODT Heading 1 and Heading 2 styles. The document contains two fictitious but realistic parties: landlord Pacific Properties LLC and tenant Meridian Analytics Inc., at a property address (4500 Technology Drive, Suite 200, Palo Alto, CA 94304) that does not appear to exist in reality. The task requires applying proper paragraph styles, adding an automatically-generated Table of Contents, and inserting page numbers in the footer per Morrison & Foerster LLP document standards.
